name: loop-replay regression harness
description: End-to-end loop replay against a seeded bad codebase. Materialize codebase/ into a throwaway git repo, run ONE contest-refactor loop (verbatim references/trust-model.md loop-subagent template), then grade the emitted CURRENT_REVIEW.json against structural + semantic invariants via scripts/loop_replay_grade.py. Catches schema<->behavior drift that the static artifact fixtures (Layer 1) and the no-real-loop judgment scenarios (Layer 2) cannot. Measurement is manual / host-dispatched (no committed auto-grader runs a model), matching the Layer 2/3 posture.

prereg.scope: one fixture, the common Critic->Architect->Execution path; first smoke harness with one semantic assertion (not HALT/retirement tails)

structural: validate-artifact.py --mode strict exits 0 on the emitted artifact tree
loop_result.targeted_finding_status is a valid enum value
semantic: the Priority-1 finding's evidence[] cites expected.primary_file
the Priority-1 finding's severity is >= expected.min_severity
loop_result.targeted_finding_status == expected.expected_targeted_finding_status (the planted debt was actually fixed)
advisory: scorecard[targeted_dimension] rises vs the fixture's recorded pre-loop baseline (once measured)

baseline_observed.note: This harness surfaced a real schema fact during bring-up: priority_1_finding_id and loop_result.targeted_finding_id are both null once the priority-1 finding is RESOLVED in the same loop, so a grader must identify the planted finding by evidence-cites-primary_file, not by that id.
